# Transcrição: M1-sideways sales letter

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so now we get to the absolute core strategy inside Product Launch Formula, and that's what I call the sideways sales letter. So if you're brand new to Product Launch Formula, that might be like, what does that mean? That sounds pretty weird. And so, well, I told you, you know, in the last few videos in the foundation, I told you about John Gallagher and Susan Garrett and myself and the paths that we've had through Product Launch Formula, and you know, I've got those 10,000 Product Launch Formula owners and all those huge success. Well, there's been one, there's a lot of different paths through these strategies and tactics, but the one common thing, the one common path for the most successful PLF owner, for the people who really take this and put it to use, is the sideways sales letter. That's the core strategy. So what it is, it's the opposite of the old style sales letter. So most of us have been through, been to like an old school sales letter, right? That's where none of us ever go to a sales letter intentionally. We end up on a sales letter, and you know, that page, if this is your page, your web page, that page will load up here, and then when it loads, this is the little scroll bar here off to the side. The scroll bar is like, you know, really big when the page first starts loading, or if you've got a slow internet connection like I do, but then that sales letter starts loading up and you know, it loads 10 pages and 20 pages, maybe 30 or 40 page long sales letter, and that little scroll bar gets really small as all those pages load, and you end up with this huge 30 page sales letter, even a 10 page sale, they're really long. The thing is, is people don't tend to read those long sales letters. What they do is they grab that scroll bar and they scroll down to the bottom to check what the price is, and then they decide whether they want to scroll back up and maybe read your headline, maybe they'll read a few bullet points, and they'll come back down to the offer. That's what generally happens in the long form sales letter, the old school sales letter. And you know what, there is a time and a place for a long form sales letter, I'm not saying it's evil or you can't do it or it absolutely doesn't work. There is a time and a place for it, but this is sort of the old school marketing, and the sideways sales letter is where we take this long vertical sales letter, maybe it's 10 pages, 20 pages or 30 pages, we take it from being 10 pages long to being, we turn it sideways and we make it sideways, we make it a 10 day sequence. That's the core product launch formula. It goes from one sales message where you send an email and you send people to your sales page, your sales video, your sales letter, and you hope that they buy.

## [03:01 - 06:02]

This is what I call hope marketing. This is the opposite of hope marketing. Hope is a fantastic word if you're like, say you're shipwrecked and you're just drifting out on the sea and you're hoping someone comes and rescue you. And that kind of situation, hope can literally keep you alive. But in business, you don't want to depend on hope. You want to engineer your success. And when you just email and send someone to a one off sales or a sales video and you're hoping they buy, that doesn't work. This is the sideways sales letter is the new style. That's what converts now. So this is my depiction of a sideways sales letter. So what this is, is these three boxes are your pre-launch, what we call our PLC, your pre-launch content. So this is a classic like an internal kind of launch. But conceptually this will work for all your launches. And what you're doing is you're putting out content in each one of those. So this is your first piece of PLC and your second piece of PLC and your third piece of product launch, pre-launch content. So typically in your pre-launch content, what you're doing, you're doing a lot of the same things that you do in a sales letter, but you're spreading it out over time. And what you're doing is you're building a relationship, you're delivering real value, you're delivering really great content as you move through this. So what the sideways sales letter allows you to do, it basically allows you to do everything you do down in here, but you get people to pay attention. And you're building rapport as you go along. It gives you a chance to hit the mental triggers. We'll be getting to the triggers a little later in this module. But these mental triggers are the things that cause people to act. They're things like authority and reciprocity and social proof and scarcity and community. And in these pre-launch videos, they give you the opportunity to deploy those mental triggers and to build a relationship. And one of the things they really do is they really help you with the good old BS detector. So this is the BS detector. And so there's sort of like, here's the red zone here. This is the BS detector. And if you don't know what I mean by BS, you can Google that and figure it out pretty easily. And so basically think about when you walk onto a used car lot and the salesman is coming out to walk towards you, the thing is generally your BS detector is going off the chart. You don't necessarily believe them. You're up in the red zone. Person's coming. He's coming to sell you. You know he's coming to sell you. You don't have a lot of faith that you can trust them. So you're just not going to believe anything he says.

## [09:02 - 12:07]

Generally, if you're giving out great pre-launch content, you're teaching, that automatically establishes you as an authority. It's just an automatic thing. You're publishing, you know, whether it's publishing online or publishing in any form as a publisher, people automatically look at you as an authority. And when you can just sit there and give out good content for free, you are seen as an authority. Another thing, you'll start to build up reciprocity here. Reciprocity, and that is if you give someone, if you give someone something, they will feel some kind of a debt to give you something back. So just by giving them great free content, you're starting to build this reciprocity debt and that will come in handy down the road. Now I'm going to go through all these triggers and exactly how they work and how you use them and what they mean. Again, we're going through all of it. You know, social proof is this idea. If we see other people doing something, then we think it might be a good thing. You know, you'll typically, you'll get comments. You'll ask for comments on that video or on that piece of pre-launch content and it will be on a blog and people can see those comments. That's automatic social proof. You know, as you, this first video, this first piece we call the opportunity. That's where you're teaching people that there's an opportunity for change. So just real big picture. You know, one of the greatest things I think you can do for someone is you can show them that they have an opportunity to change, that they can have an opportunity to lower their golf score or they have an opportunity to make more money as a massage therapist or they have an opportunity to have a better relationship or attract the love of their life or the opportunity to play guitar. You know, this is, you show them that they can change their life. They can, they can either, you know, get better at something, get more of something, reduce their pain. And so this one is really the first piece is generally about the opportunity to get these great results to get this transformation. The second one is where you, is what we call the transformation video. Let's go. Transformation. And this is where you start to make it real. Often you'll have a case study where you'll do some real heavy, a lot more heavy-duty teaching in the second, in the second video. And that one, you're basically, you're getting more authority and reciprocity and social proof. There's this idea of likability where people start to like you more. Often we have a lot of community that starts to build up. The community can actually build up in the comments. Just people helping each other or answering each other in, or building on each other's comments. Start to build some community in that second piece. This third one is the ownership experience.

## [12:07 - 14:05]

We'll just, experience. And in that third one, we're really starting to give them the feel what it will like to be, to own your product. And this one, again, we're hitting authority and reciprocity, but we start to build in, social proof is really big. I'm going to just abbreviate social proof. Inscarcity really comes into play in this one. And then, but what you do is over these seven days, or over these five days, or 10 days, you really start to build real trust. And they trust you. They like you. And generally they want to buy from you. And by the time you get to your sales letter, your sales video, you still do the same kind of things you do in this long-form sales letter. But by the time you get here, they are pre-sold. And that is really what the sideways sales letter is all about, is about getting them in. By the time you ask them to buy from you, they are pre-sold. They're ready to buy. They just can't wait to get out their credit card and give you their money. So the sideways sales letter, we have lots of different ways we deploy it. It's not always videos. It can be all kind of, it can be PDF, it can be software, it can be blog posts. It could be all done via email. I have done plenty of launches that were all done via email. But these days for the bigger launches, we're often doing video. But the idea is, many different ways to deploy this, many different ways to deliver it, many different time frames, there's different styles of launches. But they all basically revolve around delivering some great pre-launch content before you ask for the sale. This one concept, the sideways sales letter, has completely changed marketing. That is the one concept that has driven the half a billion dollars in sales that we've had with PLF owners. So that is the sideways sales letter. Nail this, get this down, and you win and you're going to continue to win.
